query: xz utils windows native mingw-w64 MSVC command-line injection vulnerability versions affected

mako.raw:
{"query":"xz utils windows native mingw-w64 MSVC command-line injection vulnerability versions affected","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1868,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://tukaani.org/xz/argument-injection.html","title":"CVE-2024-47611: Argument injection on Windows","content":"When built for native Windows (mingw-w64 or MSVC), the command line tools from XZ Utils 5.6.2 and older have a command line argument injection vulnerability (CVE-2024-47611).
Command line tools built for Cygwin or MSYS2 are unaffected. liblzma is unaffected.
If a command line contains Unicode characters (for example, filenames) that don’t exist in the current legacy code page, the characters are converted to similar-looking characters with best-fit mapping.
XZ Utils 5.6.3 (and the backported commits in the v5.4 and v5.2 branches) force the process code page to UTF-8.
The command line tools remain vulnerable if used on an old older version of Windows.
UTF-8 is now the expected encoding of the file lists read using --files and --files0 options when running on Windows 10 version 1903 or later.
If building with a mingw-w64 toolchain, it is recommended to use UCRT version instead of the old MSVCRT.
With the UTF-8 code page, messages with non-ASCII characters are not shown properly with MSVCRT.
For more information about best-fit mapping vulnerabilities, see the WorstFit website."},{"url":"https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/cve-2024-47611","title":"NVD - cve-2024-47611","content":"XZ Utils provide a general-purpose data-compression library plus command-line tools.
GitHub, Inc. https://github.com/tukaani-project/xz/security/advisories/GHSA-m538-c5qw-3cg4 [No types assigned]"},{"url":"https://panther.com/blog/cve-2024-3094-linux-supply-chain-compromise-affecting-xz-utils-data-compression-library","title":"XZ Utils Supply Chain Compromise: What to Know","content":"Panther is aware of and tracking a high-severity software supply chain vulnerability affecting the Linux library XZ Utils versions 5.6.0 and 5.6.1.
The vulnerability has been assigned CVE-2024-3094, with a CVSS score of 10 indicating the highest possible severity score.
On March 29, 2024, a supply-chain compromise was discovered in the XZ package as malicious code that could provide a backdoor into systems through this utility.
To identify if your system is impacted you can run xz -V' on the command line to see what version you are running.
By submitting this form, you acknowledge and agree that Panther will process your personal information in accordance with the Privacy Policy."},{"url":"https://bell-sw.com/blog/cve-2024-3094-a-backdoor-in-xz-utils/","title":"A critical vulnerability in the xz-utils library","content":"Apr 3, 2024 — The versions 5.6.0 and 5.6.1 are affected. The installed version of xz is 5.6.0 or 5.6.1 ・ it allows remote root access to an authenticated ...
Alpaquita Linux is not affected by this backdoor.
If you use alpaquita-linux-python or alpaquita-linux-gcc, you should immediately update the image to receive the patch."},{"url":"https://www.uptycs.com/blog/threat-research-report-team/xz-utils-backdoor-vulnerability-cve-2024-3094","title":"XZ Utils Backdoor Vulnerability (CVE-2024-3094): Comprehensive Guide","content":"On 29 March 2024, the cybersecurity community turned its attention to a newly disclosed vulnerability in XZ Utils, identified as CVE-2024-3094.
RedHat has issued a warning about this flaw in XZ Utils, a set of XZ format compression tools commonly found in Linux distributions, indicating it could potentially allow a nefarious individual to compromise sshd authentication and obtain remote unauthorized system access.
The backdoor is designed to allow a malicious actor to break the authentication and, from there, gain unauthorized access to the entire system.
Figure 2 - Detection of CVE-2024-3094 with vulnerability scan"}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"xz utils windows native mingw-w64 MSVC command-line injection vulnerability versions affected","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://tukaani.org/xz/argument-injection.html","title":"CVE-2024-47611: Argument injection on Windows","content":"# CVE-2024-47611: Argument injection on Windows. When built for native Windows (mingw-w64 or MSVC), the command line tools from XZ Utils 5.6.2 and older have a command line argument injection vulnerability (CVE-2024-47611). If a command line contains Unicode characters (for example, filenames) that don’t exist in the current legacy code page, the characters are converted to similar-looking characters with best-fit mapping. Some best-fit mappings result in ASCII characters that change the meaning of the command line, which can be exploited with malicious filenames to do argument injection or directory traversal attacks. XZ Utils 5.6.3 (and the backported commits in the v5.4 and v5.2 branches) force the process code page to UTF-8. However, forcing the process code page to UTF-8 is possible only on Windows 10 version 1903 and later. The command line tools remain vulnerable if used on an old older version of Windows. These are converted to the replacement character U+FFFD in the UTF-8 code
